query: TikTok Shop US mandatory logistics fulfillment policy rollout dates 2026
a_issues: Content A includes a featured snippet and multiple third-party pages stating the mandate would begin February 25, 2026 and phase through March 31, 2026, but it does not clearly surface the later reversal/pause as the key answer. It contains contradictory signals: one result says independent shipping remains available, while others present the mandate as if definitive. This could mislead an LLM into answering with outdated rollout dates only, despite the anchor indicating later follow-up communication and external reports showing the deadlines were paused.
b_issues: Content B is also built mostly on third-party and user-generated sources rather than primary TikTok documentation. It likewise centers the original announced rollout dates and does not include the later reversal/pause, so an LLM may still give an outdated answer. Some results are low-reliability social posts (Reddit, LinkedIn, Facebook).
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it more explicitly provides the rollout details in a consistent, specific form: new sellers onboarding on or after February 9, 2026 were to use TikTok logistics from the outset, and existing sellers were to be phased in between February 25 and March 31, 2026. Those dates appear clearly in the top results and are corroborated across multiple entries. A has similar date information, but its featured snippet states a simplified and less accurate version ('Beginning February 25, 2026' with no clear February 9 new-seller date), and its results more obviously conflict with one another due to the Easyship reversal article. Neither set is ideal because neither includes the crucial later fact that TikTok paused/reversed the mandate, but B gives the announced rollout dates more precisely and directly.
